column1: # Typoena PRODUCTION partition table — 16 MB flash (ESP32-S3-WROOM-1-N16R8).
# Dual A/B app slots for over-the-air update (`:update`). `:update` streams a new
# image into whichever slot is NOT running
# flips `otadata` to point at it; the running slot is never written
# loss mid-download is safe (the next boot still runs the current image). With
# `CONFIG_BOOTLOADER_APP_ROLLBACK_ENABLE=y` the new slot boots in "pending
# verify" and is rolled back on the next reset unless the app confirms itself
# healthy (`ota::mark_running_firmware_valid`
# This REPLACES the single-app layout for production. The one migration cost:
# a device on the old single-slot layout can't grow a second slot while running
# so it needs ONE USB reflash with this table (`just flash-ota`) to become
# OTA-capable; every update after that is over the air.
# The product git working copy lives on the SD card (`/sd/repo`)
# there is no `storage` data partition here (unlike the git-push spike's
# partitions.csv). Two 4 MB slots + otadata use ~8.5 MB of 16 MB — generous app
# headroom (the light editor is ~1 MB; the full build adds Wi-Fi + libgit2 + the
# wizard) with ~7 MB free for a future recovery slot or data partition.
# App slots MUST be equal-sized and 0x10000-aligned; otadata MUST be 0x2000.
# Name
nvs
otadata
phy_init
ota_0
ota_1
